2605.05026.title: Local Intrinsic Dimension Unveils Hallucinations in Diffusion Models
2605.05026.paper_summary: Diffusion models are prone to generating structural hallucinations - samples that match the statistical properties of the training data yet defy underlying structural rules, resulting in anomalies like hands with more than five fingers. Recent research studied this failure mode from several viewpoints, offering partial explanations to their occurrence, such as mode interpolation. In this work, we propose a complementary perspective that treats hallucinations as instabilities on the model-induced manifold. We begin by showing that a hallucination filter based on such instabilities matches or exceeds the performance of the recently proposed temporal one. By tracing the source of these instabilities, we identify local intrinsic dimension (LID) as their primary driver and propose Intrinsic Quenching (IQ), a direct corrective mechanism that deflates it to alleviate hallucinations. IQ consistently outperforms standard hallucination reduction baselines across a wide array of benchmarks and offers a highly promising solution for enforcing anatomical consistency in downstream medical imaging tasks.
2605.05026.paper_summary_zh: 擴散模型容易產生結構性幻覺——這些樣本雖然符合訓練數據的統計特性，但卻違反了基本的結構規則，導致出現異常情況，例如手指數量超過五根。最近的研究從幾個角度探討了這種失敗模式，提供了部分解釋其發生的原因，例如模式插值。在這項工作中，我們提出了一種補充的觀點，將幻覺視為模型引起的流形上的不穩定性。我們首先展示了一種基於這些不穩定性的幻覺過濾器，其性能與最近提出的時間過濾器相匹配或超過。通過追溯這些不穩定性的來源，我們確定局部內在維度（LID）是其主要驅動因素，並提出內在消退（IQ），這是一種直接的修正機制，可以減少LID以緩解幻覺。IQ在各種基準測試中始終超越標準的幻覺減少基準，並為在下游醫學影像任務中強化解剖一致性提供了一個非常有前景的解決方案。
2605.05026.author: Bartlomiej Sobieski et.al.
2605.05026.authors: Bartlomiej Sobieski, Matthew Tivnan, Dawid Płudowski, Michał Jan Włodarczyk, Pengfei Jin, Przemyslaw Biecek, Quanzheng Li
